author: ajsutton
content: You could use https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/blob-archiver - it can read blobs from any CL and store them them in a simple directory or on S3 and then exposes the same getter APIs as a CL would so it's easy to pull the data back out when needed.

author: sproul
content: this doesn't work if we don't have any working CL though, right

author: ajsutton
content: True, but you could configure CLs to prune blobs and this would cover the archive requirements for you.

author: sproul
content: I think there's still a bootstrap problem, can't seed the blob-archiver if there's no working node

author: sproul
content: our LH nodes that were blob archives are completely dead

author: sproul
content: maybe with an offline migration they could export their data to blob-archiver though

author: sproul
content: could be worth a try

author: ajsutton
content: hmm, yeah that would mean it couldn't fetch the full history.  It should be ok to start from now and archive going forward though. Of course, something has to be pushing the network forward - I'm assuming nodes would work if they weren't trying to archive blobs.

author: sproul
content: yes, it's the 4TB DB that is fatal for us

author: sproul
content: the use-case we have is that QuickNode want all archived blobs on all networks, not just from now on

author: ajsutton
content: I thought we'd been running our archiver since before blobs started on sepolia and mainnet at least. To be honest, I haven't actually looked at the status of it for quite a while though - in practice we really only need about 30 days of history in an absolute worst case.

author: sauliusgrigaitis
content: <@602753420033785856> Grandine has a new RocksDB backend, would be interesting to test with >4TB.

author: catwith1hat
content:
<@602753420033785856> I have tiered-storage implementation for Nimbus. This puts blobs into a cold storage directory. Works. I have run this for awhile on my Hoodi node. It works using the `ATTACH DATABASE` feature from sqlite.

I didn't get to open a PR yet.

https://github.com/catwith1hat/nimbus-eth2/commit/5f3416205efee6203b2b2e0f874fa287c6eab1d7 + https://github.com/status-im/nim-eth/commit/0e932a260ee624efcdb2da97d41b2d312812f8f6

author: barnabasbusa
content: is it possible to put blobs in a different datadir in grandine? E.g shove blobs into hdd instead of nvme?

author: potuz
content: Afaik Prysm stores directly in the local filesystem in a directory. I think we have a flag to not prune them simply and that's it

author: sauliusgrigaitis
content: Not yet, is it something that other users need?

author: barnabasbusa
content: well if one wants to run an archive blob storage solution, it would be pretty good I think

author: barnabasbusa
content: its the main reason we run lighthouse only for blob archive

author: barnabasbusa
content: otherwise it would get way too expensive

author: sproul
content: problem with that <@412614104222531604> is that the HDD write is currently on the hot path for LH block import. Once that HDD write takes >12s, sync is dead. This is what happened to several of the LH users running blob archives https://github.com/sigp/lighthouse/issues/9214

author: sproul
content: (to be clear, the DB design is so bad that HDD/NVMe would both be dead in the water with our current code)

author: barnabasbusa
content: can't you keep unfinalized blob data in memory, and shove finalized blocks into disk?

author: sproul
content: many things one _can_ do, but at the moment we do this and it’s bad

author: sproul
content: we need time to fix it

author: tersec
content: oh can make a PR based on this in e2store, was discussing this with Lion recently

author: tersec
content: but right now it's not implemented in production Nimbus no

content: <@654267572107083777> Is there an interest in the patch, I posted above?
